steps.title: Identify the core prediction
steps.content: The researcher claims that as human populations migrated farther from Africa, each new language retained progressively fewer sounds from the original language. This predicts a correlation: distance from Africa should correlate with fewer sounds in modern languages.

steps.title: Test the prediction against the choices
steps.content:
B directly contradicts the predicted pattern: languages in Europe and Western Asia (closer to Africa) have more sounds than languages in Africa, reversing the expected correlation.

The other options either support the claim or don't test the distance-sound relationship.

B is correct.
